Title: Re-routing plant terpene biosynthesis enables momilactone pathway elucidation

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Momilactone B has allelopathic activity and inhibits Arabidopsis seed germination and root growth; forskolin is a hedgehog signal inhibitor; taxadiene is a taxol intermediate
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of diterpene scaffolds and downstream diterpenoids in the cytosolic mevalonate-engineered system
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HMGR was co-expressed as the rate-limiting mevalonate-pathway enzyme to boost cytosolic precursor supply for diterpene production in N. benthamiana. 2. Co-expression of HMGR with cytosolic GGPPS and pathway synthases increased yields of taxadiene, forskolin intermediates/products, and momilactone pathway intermediates/products. 3. HMGR is an enabling engineering component for pathway re-routing rather than a discovered momilactone-specific biosynthetic step.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: cytGGPPS; OsCPS4; OsKSL4; CfTPS2; CfTPS3; TDS
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; metabolite profiling by GC-MS
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful chassis-engineering component to increase cytosolic isoprenoid flux for multigene diterpenoid pathway reconstruction in plants
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as an auxiliary flux-boosting gene, not a native momilactone pathway gene under discovery
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT expression system; promoter type NA
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression with cytGGPPS altered endogenous triterpene metabolism

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Momilactone B is allelopathic; forskolin is a hedgehog inhibitor; taxadiene is a taxol intermediate
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: cytGGPPS increases GGPP supply and is required with HMGR for efficient cytosolic diterpene production
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. N-terminal truncation of chloroplast-targeted GGPPS partially redirected localization away from plastids. 2. cytGGPPS was required together with HMGR and cytosolic diterpene synthases for major yield improvements in taxadiene, forskolin precursor 13, and syn-pimaradiene. 3. cytGGPPS functions as precursor-supplying engineering module rather than a newly elucidated momilactone-specific pathway step.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: HMGR; TDS; OsCPS4; OsKSL4; CfTPS2; CfTPS3
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression; GFP localization; confocal microscopy; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-supply module for plant diterpene pathway engineering via cytosolic rerouting
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression of cytGGPPS and HMGR altered endogenous triterpene metabolism; source species identity for different GGPPS constructs is not fully unified in text
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT expression system; promoter type NA
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast for native chlGGPPS; partially cytosolic after truncation

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Allelopathic inhibitor of competing plants; inhibits Arabidopsis seed germination and root growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required to initiate momilactone scaffold biosynthesis and, in cytosolic rerouting, increases syn-pimaradiene and downstream momilactone production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCPS4 is one of the requisite diterpene synthases for momilactone scaffold formation. 2. Cytosolic rerouting of OsCPS4 together with OsKSL4, cytGGPPS, and HMGR gave ~10-fold increased syn-pimaradiene relative to plastidial expression. 3. OsCPS4 is essential for complete reconstitution of momilactone B biosynthesis in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranylgeranyl pyrophosphate to syn-copalyl diphosphate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms precursor to the momilactone diterpene scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; pathway reconstitution; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Co-expression mining using rice GEO dataset GSE18361 identified pathway candidates using OsCPS4 as bait
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast-targeted natively; cytosolic after N-terminal truncation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves; rice roots under Magnaporthe oryzae elicitation used for co-expression mining
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core entry enzyme for transfer of momilactone biosynthesis into heterologous plant hosts
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed in rice roots under fungal elicitation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT expression system; promoter type NA

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Allelopathic inhibitor of competing plants; inhibits Arabidopsis seed germination and root growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required with OsCPS4 for formation of syn-pimaradiene scaffold leading to momilactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsKSL4 acts with OsCPS4 to generate syn-pimaradiene (1), the momilactone scaffold precursor. 2. Cytosolic rerouting with HMGR and cytGGPPS markedly improved syn-pimaradiene production. 3. OsKSL4 is essential for complete momilactone B pathway reconstitution in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct scaffold precursor for downstream oxidations en route to momilactone B
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; pathway reconstitution; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Co-expression mining using rice GEO dataset GSE18361 identified pathway candidates using OsKSL4 as bait
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast-targeted natively; cytosolic after N-terminal truncation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves; rice roots under Magnaporthe oryzae elicitation used for co-expression mining
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core scaffold-forming enzyme for engineering allelopathic momilactone production in crops
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed in rice roots under fungal elicitation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT expression system; promoter type NA

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Oxidizes the syn-pimaradiene scaffold toward syn-pimaradien-19-oic acid and is required for downstream momilactone pathway progression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP99A3 converted syn-pimaradiene-derived scaffold to syn-pimaradien-19-oic acid (2) in the engineered N. benthamiana system. 2. Its activity matched prior single-enzyme characterization and was reproduced in planta after cytosolic rerouting. 3. In combination with CYP76M8 and downstream enzymes, CYP99A3 contributes to formation of later oxidized intermediates en route to momilactone B.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes the C19 methyl of syn-pimaradiene through alcohol/aldehyde to syn-pimaradien-19-oic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms key oxidized intermediate en route to target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Co-expression mining using rice GEO dataset GSE18361 included CYP99A3 as bait
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary oxidative tailoring enzyme for reconstructing momilactone pathway in heterologous plants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed in rice roots under fungal elicitation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Precise order with CYP76M8 and endogenous N. benthamiana oxidation remains unresolved according to Discussion
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT expression system; promoter type NA

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hydroxylates the momilactone scaffold and, with CYP99A3 and OsMAS, enables lactone-containing intermediate formation en route to momilactone B
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76M8 individually produced syn-pimaradien-6-ol (3) in the engineered system. 2. When combined with CYP99A3, it reduced levels of 2 and generated multiple more oxidized intermediates (4 and 5). 3. CYP76M8 is required with OsMAS for production of lactone intermediate 6 and for complete downstream pathway progression.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates syn-pimaradiene at C6 to form syn-pimaradien-6-ol; also contributes to later oxidized intermediates in pathway context
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms parallel oxidized intermediate branch that feeds target product formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Previously characterized enzyme; identified among co-expressed candidates in rice-related analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential oxidative tailoring gene for heterologous momilactone pathway transfer
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously characterized rice diterpenoid-pathway gene; induced/co-expressed with momilactone genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact order of oxidation events with CYP99A3 remains unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT expression system; promoter type NA

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Necessary for production of lactone intermediate 6 and supports conversion of oxidized intermediates toward momilactone A/B branch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Addition of OsMAS to the CYP99A3+CYP76M8 module was necessary for production of lactone 6. 2. OsMAS reduced levels of intermediates 4 and 5, consistent with conversion toward downstream pathway metabolites. 3. Discussion notes uncertainty whether OsMAS contributes to oxidation leading to ketone 8 in conjunction with CYP701A8.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidation/rearrangement steps associated with formation of the lactone-containing intermediate 6 and previously implicated in momilactone A formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: oxidized syn-pimaradiene intermediates 4/5 or related precursors
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate downstream intermediate toward momilactone A/B
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Co-expression mining using rice GEO dataset GSE18361 included OsMAS as bait
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required downstream tailoring enzyme for heterologous momilactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed in rice roots under fungal elicitation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely an SDR-type oxidoreductase; exact step order relative to CYP701A8 remains unresolved in Discussion
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT expression system; promoter type NA
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Short-chain dehydrogenase/reductase (inferred from prior pathway context)

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Oxidizes intermediate 6 toward the C3 ketone intermediate 8 and contributes to momilactone B formation when combined with CYP76M14
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Screening identified CYP701A8 as a candidate that oxidizes lactone intermediate 6 to the C3 ketone 8. 2. CYP701A8 had previously been shown to oxidize syn-pimaradiene to syn-pimaradien-3-ol. 3. Co-expression of CYP701A8 with CYP76M14 in the full pathway enabled production of momilactone B.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late oxidative tailoring toward momilactone B
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes intermediate 6 at C3 toward ketone intermediate 8, possibly via a C3 alcohol intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: intermediate 8 (momilactone A) or preceding C3-hydroxylated intermediate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor to target product branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate screening by transient expression in N. benthamiana; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Identified among co-expressed candidate genes from rice dataset GSE18361
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Late-pathway oxidation gene needed for complete heterologous momilactone B production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with rice momilactone genes in elicited roots
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion states exact oxidation sequence between CYP701A8 and OsMAS is unresolved; low background formation without CYP701A8 suggests possible endogenous interference
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT expression system; promoter type NA

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Oxidizes intermediate 6 to the C20 hydroxy intermediate 7 and, with CYP701A8, enables momilactone B production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76M14 was identified from candidate screening and is closely related to CYP76M8. 2. In the reconstructed pathway, CYP76M14 converts 6 to the C20 hydroxy intermediate 7. 3. Co-expression of CYP76M14 with CYP701A8 in the full pathway produced momilactone B.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late oxidative tailoring toward momilactone B
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates intermediate 6 at C20 to form intermediate 7
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor branch toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate screening by transient expression in N. benthamiana; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Identified as co-expressed candidate from rice dataset GSE18361
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key late-pathway gene for completing momilactone B biosynthesis in heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expression candidate in elicited rice roots; specialized diterpenoid biosynthesis-associated
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Formation of momilactone B likely involves spontaneous hemiacetal ring closure after C20 hydroxylation and C3 oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT expression system; promoter type NA
